mnemonics: PROFIT (Protocols, Routing, OSI layers, Firewalls, IP addressing, Topologies) - A quick acronym to recall major topics in Computer Networks.
keyPoints: The OSI model has 7 layers (Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application) which define networking functions.
TCP is a connection-oriented protocol that ensures reliable data delivery, while UDP is connectionless and used for faster, but less reliable, transmission.
IP addressing (like IPv4 and IPv6) provides unique identifiers for devices on a network, enabling communication and routing.
commonMistakes: Confusing the functions of different OSI layers, e.g., thinking routing happens at the Data Link layer instead of the Network layer.
Assuming a firewall operates only at the application layer; modern firewalls can filter traffic at multiple layers (packet filtering, stateful inspection, application-layer).
